task_completion.description: Open the 'dwanko_run.tnz' sample scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz interface is visible with the 'dwanko_run' scene loaded, verifiable by looking at the window title bar, xsheet, or timeline.

task_completion.description: Configure the camera for a static wide shot for the first 12 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The viewer displays a wide shot of the character. The camera pegbar or timeline shows an initial keyframe with no subsequent keyframes altering the camera position or zoom between frames 1 and 12.

task_completion.description: Create a hard camera cut exactly at Frame 13.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The xsheet/timeline shows a new camera keyframe exactly at frame 13. The interpolation for the camera movement must be set to 'step' or 'constant' (or identical keyframes placed at frame 12 and 13) to ensure an instantaneous transition without smooth interpolation.

task_completion.description: Configure the camera for a static close-up (at least 200% zoom centered on the character) for frames 13-24.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The viewer displays a close-up of the character (significantly zoomed in compared to the wide shot) for frames 13-24. The timeline shows no camera movement keyframes after frame 13.

task_completion.description: Set the render output directory to /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/camera_cut/ and the format to PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/camera_cut/' and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render frames 1-24 as a PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, or a file explorer/terminal window shows the generated PNG files (e.g., 0001.png to 0024.png) in the target output directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to configure the camera and render the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with OpenToonz menus, the timeline, and camera settings, rather than using terminal scripts to modify project files directly.

integrity.description: The rendered PNG files were generated by OpenToonz's render engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render dialog or progress bar is shown. There is no evidence of terminal commands using ImageMagick or other external tools to artificially crop or duplicate images.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided 'dwanko_run.tnz' scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz viewer shows the actual dwanko character animation, and the xsheet shows the animation levels for the run cycle, rather than a fake scene or a static image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source scene: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/camera_cut. Expected frame count: 24. Cut frame index: 12 (0-indexed, which corresponds to Frame 13 in the UI). The verifier checks for a zoom threshold ratio of 1.3 and a stability variance threshold of 0.2 to validate the instant camera cut.
